title: Add production caching and refresh for GitHub artifacts
objective: Reduce GitHub API traffic and improve application responsiveness by caching the private repository artifact catalogue while keeping refresh behaviour predictable.
userContext: Reading a repository tree and downloading multiple Markdown files on every page request would be slow and consume GitHub API quota. The user also needs a clear way to know when content was last refreshed and to request fresh data.
currentBehaviour: DATA-002 reads artifacts directly from GitHub. There is no persistent production cache, last-refresh metadata, stale-data fallback or manual refresh action.
requiredBehaviour: The application must cache the parsed artifact catalogue server-side, detect repository changes, refresh predictably and provide safe stale-data behaviour during temporary GitHub failures.
userExperience: The library loads quickly. The user can see when artifacts were last refreshed and can request a refresh. Newly committed artifacts become visible within a documented period or after manual refresh. Temporary GitHub outages do not unnecessarily make previously loaded artifacts disappear.
functionalRequirements: Add a server-side cache for the parsed artifact catalogue.
Key cached content by repository, branch and repository revision or commit SHA.
Define and implement a cache freshness period.
Check for repository changes efficiently before downloading all files.
Add a protected manual refresh action.
Invalidate or replace the cache after successful repository writes.
Serve stale cached content during temporary GitHub API failures where safe.
Clearly indicate stale data and last refresh time.
Do not serve cached artifact data to unauthorised users.
Avoid concurrent duplicate refreshes where practical.
Document expected refresh timing and failure behaviour.
technicalConsiderations: Prefer Cloudflare-compatible storage such as KV or Cache API.
Avoid introducing D1 unless richer querying is demonstrably required.
Keep cache content scoped to the configured private repository.
Do not put access tokens or session data in cache entries.
Use commit SHA or equivalent revision data for deterministic invalidation.
Ensure tests can use a local or mocked cache implementation.
outOfScope: Full-text search indexing outside the current client-side search behaviour.
acceptanceCriteria: Repeated library requests do not redownload every artifact file unnecessarily.
The cache is invalidated or refreshed when the repository revision changes.
A manual refresh action is available to authorised users.
Stale cached content can be served during temporary GitHub failures with a clear indication.
Unauthorised users cannot read cached artifact content.
Cache configuration and lifecycle are documented.
Relevant tests and Cloudflare deployment validation pass.
The current application specification is updated for cache and refresh behaviour.
The implementation pull request includes DATA-003 and closes the generated issue.
codexGuidance: Build on DATA-002. Keep the initial design small and Cloudflare-native. Prefer a catalogue cache over caching individual UI pages.
